   107→func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {
   108→	return &Connection{
   109→		Connection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),
   110→	}
   111→}
   112→
   113→func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {
   114→	return conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)
   115→}
   116→
   117→func (conn *Connection) Close() {
   118→	conn.s.closeConnection(conn)
   119→}
   120→
   121→func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {
   122→	return conn.proxy
   123→}
   124→
   125→func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {
   126→	return conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)
   127→}
   128→
   129→func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {
   130→	pushEv := ev.(*Event)
   131→	err := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)
   132→	pushEv.done()
   133→	return err
   134→}
   135→
   136→// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also
   137→// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex
   138→// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.
   139→func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {
   140→	stype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)
   141→	log.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s ", stype,
   142→		con.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)
   143→	if req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {
   144→		s.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)
   145→		return nil
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.
   149→	if strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {
   150→		return s.pushXds(con,
   151→			&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},
   152→			&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	shouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)
   156→	if !shouldRespond {
   157→		return nil
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	request := &model.PushRequest{
   161→		Full:   true,
   162→		Push:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,
   163→		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),
   164→
   165→		// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time
   166→		// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,
   167→		// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.
   168→		Start:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,
   169→		Delta:  delta,
   170→		Forced: true,
   171→	}
   172→
   173→	// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.
   174→	// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),
   175→	// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope
   176→	// computation.
   177→	if con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {
   178→		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)
   179→	}
   180→	return s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)
   181→}
   182→
   183→// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.
   184→func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {
   185→	return s.Stream(stream)
   186→}
   187→
   188→func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {
   189→	if knativeEnv != "" && firstRequest.Load() {
   190→		// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the "loading" request. During
   191→		// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.
   192→		// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a
   193→		// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.
   194→		firstRequest.Store(false)
   195→		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server warmup not complete; try again")
   196→	}
   197→	// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.
   198→	// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build
   199→	// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would
   200→	// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing
   201→	// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding
   202→	// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy
   203→	// ip tables update latencies.
   204→	// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.
   205→	if !s.IsServerReady() {
   206→		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server is not ready to serve discovery information")
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	ctx := stream.Context()
   210→	peerAddr := "0.0.0.0"
   211→	if peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {
   212→		peerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()
   213→	}
   214→
   215→	if err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {
   216→		log.Warnf("ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v", peerAddr, err)
   217→		return status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, "request rate limit exceeded: %v", err)
   218→	}
   219→
   220→	ids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)
   221→	if err != nil {
   222→		return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
   223→	}
   224→	if ids != nil {
   225→		log.Debugf("Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v", peerAddr, ids)
   226→	} else {
   227→		log.Debugf("Unauthenticated XDS: %s", peerAddr)
   228→	}
   229→
   230→	// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.
   231→	s.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)
   232→	con := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)
   233→	con.ids = ids
   234→	con.s = s
   235→	return xds.Stream(con)
   236→}
   237→
   238→// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection
   239→// to the tracking map.
   240→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {
   241→	// Setup the initial proxy metadata
   242→	proxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)
   243→	if err != nil {
   244→		return err
   245→	}
   246→	// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.
   247→	if alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {
   248→		proxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias
   249→	}
   250→	// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This
   251→	// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.
   252→	proxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()
   253→	// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.
   254→	con.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))
   255→	con.node = node
   256→	con.proxy = proxy
   257→	if proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {
   258→		return fmt.Errorf("ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true")
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	// Authorize xds clients
   262→	if err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {
   263→		return err
   264→	}
   265→
   266→	// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of
   267→	// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like
   268→	// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push
   269→	// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new
   270→	// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.
   271→	s.addCon(con.ID(), con)
   272→	// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the
   273→	// proxy
   274→	defer con.MarkInitialized()
   275→
   276→	// Complete full initialization of the proxy
   277→	if err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {
   278→		s.closeConnection(con)
   279→		return err
   280→	}
   281→	return nil
   282→}
   283→
   284→func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {
   285→	if con.ID() == "" {
   286→		return
   287→	}
   288→	s.removeCon(con.ID())
   289→	s.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)
   290→}
   291→
   292→func connectionID(node string) string {
   293→	id := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)
   294→	return node + "-" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)
   295→}
   296→
   297→// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from
   298→// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to
   299→// fully initialize the proxy.
   300→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {
   301→	meta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)
   302→	if err != nil {
   303→		return nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()
   304→	}
   305→	proxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)
   306→	if err != nil {
   307→		return nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()
   308→	}
   309→	// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy
   310→	proxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)
   311→	proxy.XdsNode = node
   312→	return proxy, nil
   313→}
   314→
   315→// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label
   316→// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.
   317→func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {
   318→	// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to
   319→	// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this
   320→	// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,
   321→	// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).
   322→	proxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)
   323→	if proxy.Locality == nil {
   324→		// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.
   325→		// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it
   326→		// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.
   327→		proxy.Locality = &core.Locality{
   328→			Region:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),
   329→			Zone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),
   330→			SubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),
   331→		}
   332→	}
   333→	// add topology labels to proxy labels
   334→	proxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(
   335→		proxy.Labels,
   336→		proxy.Metadata.ClusterID,
   337→		util.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),
   338→		proxy.GetNodeName(),
   339→		proxy.Metadata.Network,
   340→	)
   341→}
   342→
   343→func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {
   344→	region, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]
   345→	zone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]
   346→	subzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]
   347→	if !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {
   348→		// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)
   349→		// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.
   350→		localityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)
   351→		if localityLabel != "" {
   352→			return util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)
   353→		}
   354→		return nil
   355→	}
   356→	return &core.Locality{
   357→		Region:  region,
   358→		Zone:    zone,
   359→		SubZone: subzone,
   360→	}
   361→}
   362→
   363→// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after
   364→// initProxyMetadata.
   365→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {
   366→	proxy := con.proxy
   367→	// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata
   368→	// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod
   369→	if err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {
   370→		return err
   371→	}
   372→	s.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)
   373→	// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.
   374→	proxy.DiscoverIPMode()
   375→
   376→	proxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}
   377→	// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.
   378→	if proxy.Metadata.Generator != "" {
   379→		proxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]
   380→	}
   381→
   382→	return nil
   383→}
   384→
   385→func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {
   386→	proxy.Lock()
   387→	defer proxy.Unlock()
   388→	var shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool
   389→	// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.
   390→	// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy
   391→	if request == nil || request.Forced ||
   392→		proxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {
   393→		proxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)
   394→		// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,
   395→		// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.
   396→		shouldResetGateway = true
   397→	}
   398→
   399→	// only recompute workload labels when
   400→	// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization
   401→	// 2. proxy update
   402→	recomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()
   403→	if recomputeLabels {
   404→		proxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)
   405→		setTopologyLabels(proxy)
   406→	}
   407→	// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.
   408→	// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still
   409→	// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become
   410→	// applicable to this proxy
   411→	push := proxy.LastPushContext
   412→	if request == nil {
   413→		shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   414→	} else {
   415→		push = request.Push
   416→		if request.Forced {
   417→			shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   418→		}
   419→		for conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {
   420→			switch conf.Kind {
   421→			case kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:
   422→				shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   423→			case kind.Gateway:
   424→				shouldResetGateway = true
   425→			case kind.Ingress:
   426→				shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   427→				shouldResetGateway = true
   428→			}
   429→			if shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {
   430→				break
   431→			}
   432→		}
   433→	}
   434→	// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.
   435→	if shouldResetSidecarScope {
   436→		proxy.SetSidecarScope(push)
   437→	}
   438→	// only compute gateways for "router" type proxy.
   439→	if shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {
   440→		proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)
   441→	}
   442→	proxy.LastPushContext = push
   443→	if request != nil {
   444→		proxy.LastPushTime = request.Start
   445→	}
   446→}
   447→
   448→// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.
   449→func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {
   450→	if features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {
   451→		event := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}
   452→		event.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil
   453→		if !event.Healthy {
   454→			event.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message
   455→		}
   456→		s.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)
   457→	}
   458→}
   459→
   460→// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.
   461→// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.
   462→// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this
   463→// pattern.
   464→//
   465→// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full
   466→// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.
   467→func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {
   468→	return s.StreamDeltas(stream)
   469→}
   470→
   471→// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.
   472→func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {
   473→	pushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest
   474→
   475→	if pushRequest.Full {
   476→		// Update Proxy with current information.
   477→		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)
   478→	}
   479→
   480→	pushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)
   481→	if !needsPush {
   482→		log.Debugf("Skipping push to %v, no updates required", con.ID())
   483→		return nil
   484→	}
   485→
   486→	// Send pushes to all generators
   487→	// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push
   488→	wrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()
   489→	for _, w := range wrl {
   490→		if err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {
   491→			return err
   492→		}
   493→	}
   494→	proxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())
   495→	return nil
   496→}
   497→
   498→// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random
   499→// order after the types listed here
   500→var PushOrder = []string{
   501→	v3.ClusterType,
   502→	v3.EndpointType,
   503→	v3.ListenerType,
   504→	v3.RouteType,
   505→	v3.SecretType,
   506→	v3.AddressType,
   507→	v3.WorkloadType,
   508→	v3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,
   509→}
   510→
   511→// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.
   512→var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)
   513→
   514→func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {
   515→	s.adsClientsMutex.RLock()
   516→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()
   517→	return len(s.adsClients)
   518→}
   519→
   520→func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {
   521→	var connection *Connection
   522→
   523→	for _, v := range s.Clients() {
   524→		if v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {
   525→			connection = v
   526→			break
   527→		}
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot
   531→	if connection == nil {
   532→		return
   533→	}
   534→	if log.DebugEnabled() {
   535→		currentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()
   536→		if currentlyPending != 0 {
   537→			log.Debugf("Starting new push while %v were still pending", currentlyPending)
   538→		}
   539→	}
   540→
   541→	s.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{
   542→		Full:   true,
   543→		Push:   s.globalPushContext(),
   544→		Start:  time.Now(),
   545→		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),
   546→		Forced: true,
   547→	})
   548→}
   549→
   550→// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.
   551→// Mainly used in Debug interface.
   552→func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {
   553→	s.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{
   554→		Full:   true,
   555→		Push:   s.globalPushContext(),
   556→		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),
   557→		Forced: true,
   558→	})
   559→}
   560→
   561→// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.
   562→func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {
   563→	if !req.Full {
   564→		log.Infof("XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s",
   565→			s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)
   566→	} else {
   567→		totalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())
   568→		log.Infof("XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s",
   569→			totalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)
   570→		monServices.Record(float64(totalService))
   571→
   572→		// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists
   573→		if req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {
   574→			req.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])
   575→		}
   576→	}
   577→
   578→	s.StartPush(req)
   579→}
   580→
   581→// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.
   582→func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {
   583→	// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.
   584→	if log.DebugEnabled() {
   585→		currentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()
   586→		if currentlyPending != 0 {
   587→			log.Debugf("Starting new push while %v were still pending", currentlyPending)
   588→		}
   589→	}
   590→	req.Start = time.Now()
   591→	for _, p := range s.AllClients() {
   592→		s.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)
   593→	}
   594→}
   595→
   596→func (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {
   597→	s.adsClientsMutex.Lock()
   598→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()
   599→	s.adsClients[conID] = con
   600→	recordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)
   601→}
   602→
   603→func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {
   604→	s.adsClientsMutex.Lock()
   605→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()
   606→
   607→	if con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {
   608→		log.Errorf("ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.", conID)
   609→		xds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()
   610→	} else {
   611→		delete(s.adsClients, conID)
   612→		recordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)
   613→	}
   614→}
   615→
   616→func (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {
   617→	return conn.proxy.Clusters()
   618→}
   619→
   620→// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of
   621→// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.
   622→func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {
   623→	allWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()
   624→	ordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))
   625→	// first add all known types, in order
   626→	for _, tp := range PushOrder {
   627→		if allWatched[tp] != nil {
   628→			ordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])
   629→		}
   630→	}
   631→	// Then add any undeclared types
   632→	for tp, res := range allWatched {
   633→		if !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {
   634→			ordered = append(ordered, res)
   635→		}
   636→	}
   637→	return ordered
   638→}

    15→package xds
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"strings"
    19→	"time"
    20→
    21→	core "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3"
    22→	discovery "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3"
    23→	"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
    24→	"google.golang.org/grpc/status"
    25→	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb"
    26→
    27→	"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features"
    28→	istiogrpc "istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc"
    29→	"istio.io/istio/pkg/model"
    30→	"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets"
    31→)
    32→
    33→// ResourceDelta records the difference in requested resources by an XDS client
    34→type ResourceDelta struct {
    35→	// Subscribed indicates the client requested these additional resources
    36→	Subscribed sets.String
    37→	// Unsubscribed indicates the client no longer requires these resources
    38→	Unsubscribed sets.String
    39→}
    40→
    41→var emptyResourceDelta = ResourceDelta{}
    42→
    43→func (rd ResourceDelta) IsEmpty() bool {
    44→	return len(rd.Subscribed) == 0 && len(rd.Unsubscribed) == 0
    45→}
    46→
    47→type Resources = []*discovery.Resource
    48→
    49→func ResourcesToAny(r Resources) []*anypb.Any {
    50→	a := make([]*anypb.Any, 0, len(r))
    51→	for _, rr := range r {
    52→		a = append(a, rr.Resource)
    53→	}
    54→	return a
    55→}
    56→
    57→// WatchedResource tracks an active DiscoveryRequest subscription.
    58→type WatchedResource struct {
    59→	// TypeUrl is copied from the DiscoveryRequest.TypeUrl that initiated watching this resource.
    60→	// nolint
    61→	TypeUrl string
    62→
    63→	// ResourceNames tracks the list of resources that are actively watched.
    64→	// For LDS and CDS, all resources of the TypeUrl type are watched if it is empty.
    65→	// For endpoints the resource names will have list of clusters and for clusters it is empty.
    66→	// For Delta Xds, all resources of the TypeUrl that a client has subscribed to.
    67→	ResourceNames sets.String
    68→
    69→	// Wildcard indicates the subscription is a wildcard subscription. This only applies to types that
    70→	// allow both wildcard and non-wildcard subscriptions.
    71→	Wildcard bool
    72→
    73→	// NonceSent is the nonce sent in the last sent response. If it is equal with NonceAcked, the
    74→	// last message has been processed. If empty: we never sent a message of this type.
    75→	NonceSent string
    76→
    77→	// NonceAcked is the last acked message.
    78→	NonceAcked string
    79→
    80→	// AlwaysRespond, if true, will ensure that even when a request would otherwise be treated as an
    81→	// ACK, it will be responded to. This typically happens when a proxy reconnects to another instance of
    82→	// Istiod. In that case, Envoy expects us to respond to EDS/RDS/SDS requests to finish warming of
    83→	// clusters/listeners.
    84→	// Typically, this should be set to 'false' after response; keeping it true would likely result in an endless loop.
    85→	AlwaysRespond bool
    86→
    87→	// LastSendTime tracks the last time we sent a message. This should change every time NonceSent changes.
    88→	LastSendTime time.Time
    89→
    90→	// LastError records the last error returned, if any. This is cleared on any successful ACK.
    91→	LastError string
    92→
    93→	// LastResources tracks the contents of the last push.
    94→	// This field is extremely expensive to maintain and is typically disabled
    95→	LastResources Resources
    96→}
    97→
    98→type Watcher interface {
    99→	DeleteWatchedResource(url string)
   100→	GetWatchedResource(url string) *WatchedResource
   101→	NewWatchedResource(url string, names []string)
   102→	UpdateWatchedResource(string, func(*WatchedResource) *WatchedResource)
   103→	// GetID identifies an xDS client. This is different from a connection ID.
   104→	GetID() string
   105→}
   106→
   107→// IsWildcardTypeURL checks whether a given type is a wildcard type
   108→// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#how-the-client-specifies-what-resources-to-return
   109→// If the list of resource names becomes empty, that means that the client is no
   110→// longer interested in any resources of the specified type. For Listener and
   111→// Cluster resource types, there is also a “wildcard” mode, which is triggered
   112→// when the initial request on the stream for that resource type contains no
   113→// resource names.
   114→func IsWildcardTypeURL(typeURL string) bool {
   115→	switch typeURL {
   116→	case model.SecretType, model.EndpointType, model.RouteType, model.ExtensionConfigurationType:
   117→		// By XDS spec, these are not wildcard
   118→		return false
   119→	case model.ClusterType, model.ListenerType:
   120→		// By XDS spec, these are wildcard
   121→		return true
   122→	default:
   123→		// All of our internal types use wildcard semantics
   124→		return true
   125→	}
   126→}
   127→
   128→// DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.
   129→type DiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesServer
   130→
   131→// Connection holds information about an xDS client connection. There may be more than one connection to the same client.
   132→type Connection struct {
   133→	// peerAddr is the address of the client, from network layer.
   134→	peerAddr string
   135→
   136→	// Time of connection, for debugging
   137→	connectedAt time.Time
   138→
   139→	// conID is the connection conID, used as a key in the connection table.
   140→	// Currently based on the node name and a counter.
   141→	conID string
   142→
   143→	// Sending on this channel results in a push.
   144→	pushChannel chan any
   145→
   146→	// Both ADS and SDS streams implement this interface
   147→	stream DiscoveryStream
   148→
   149→	// initialized channel will be closed when proxy is initialized. Pushes, or anything accessing
   150→	// the proxy, should not be started until this channel is closed.
   151→	initialized chan struct{}
   152→
   153→	// stop can be used to end the connection manually via debug endpoints. Only to be used for testing.
   154→	stop chan struct{}
   155→
   156→	// reqChan is used to receive discovery requests for this connection.
   157→	reqChan chan *discovery.DiscoveryRequest
   158→
   159→	// errorChan is used to process error during discovery request processing.
   160→	errorChan chan error
   161→}
   162→
   163→func NewConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) Connection {
   164→	return Connection{
   165→		pushChannel: make(chan any),
   166→		initialized: make(chan struct{}),
   167→		stop:        make(chan struct{}),
   168→		reqChan:     make(chan *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, 1),
   169→		errorChan:   make(chan error, 1),
   170→		peerAddr:    peerAddr,
   171→		connectedAt: time.Now(),
   172→		stream:      stream,
   173→	}
   174→}

   176→func (conn *Connection) InitializedCh() chan struct{} {

   178→}

   180→func (conn *Connection) PushCh() chan any {

   182→}

   184→func (conn *Connection) StopCh() chan struct{} {

   186→}

   188→func (conn *Connection) ErrorCh() chan error {

   220→// ConnectionContext is used by the RPC event loop to respond to requests and pushes.
   221→type ConnectionContext interface {
   222→	XdsConnection() *Connection
   223→	Watcher() Watcher
   224→	// Initialize checks the first request.
   225→	Initialize(node *core.Node) error
   226→	// Close discards the connection.
   227→	Close()
   228→	// Process responds to a discovery request.
   229→	Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error
   230→	// Push responds to a push event queue
   231→	Push(ev any) error
   232→}
   233→
   234→func Stream(ctx ConnectionContext) error {
   235→	con := ctx.XdsConnection()
   236→	// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected
   237→	// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions
   238→	// with push. According to the spec: "It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important
   239→	// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent."
   240→
   241→	// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.
   242→	// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().
   243→	go Receive(ctx)
   244→
   245→	// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because
   246→	// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout
   247→	// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to
   248→	// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the
   249→	// initialization is complete.
   250→	<-con.initialized
   251→
   252→	for {
   253→		// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.
   254→		// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)
   255→		// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.
   256→		select {
   257→		case req, ok := <-con.reqChan:
   258→			if ok {
   259→				if err := ctx.Process(req); err != nil {
   260→					return err
   261→				}
   262→			} else {
   263→				// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.
   264→				return <-con.errorChan
   265→			}
   266→		case <-con.stop:
   267→			return nil
   268→		default:
   269→		}
   270→		// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge
   271→		// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;
   272→		// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests
   273→		// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.
   274→		select {
   275→		case req, ok := <-con.reqChan:
   276→			if ok {
   277→				if err := ctx.Process(req); err != nil {
   278→					return err
   279→				}
   280→			} else {
   281→				// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.
   282→				return <-con.errorChan
   283→			}
   284→		case pushEv := <-con.pushChannel:
   285→			err := ctx.Push(pushEv)
   286→			if err != nil {
   287→				return err
   288→			}
   289→		case <-con.stop:
   290→			return nil
   291→		}
   292→	}
   293→}
   294→
   295→func Receive(ctx ConnectionContext) {
   296→	con := ctx.XdsConnection()
   297→	defer func() {
   298→		close(con.errorChan)
   299→		close(con.reqChan)
   300→		// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream.
   301→		select {
   302→		case <-con.initialized:
   303→		default:
   304→			close(con.initialized)
   305→		}
   306→	}()
   307→
   308→	firstRequest := true
   309→	for {
   310→		req, err := con.stream.Recv()
   311→		if err != nil {
   312→			if istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {
   313→				log.Infof("ADS: %q %s terminated", con.peerAddr, con.conID)
   314→				return
   315→			}
   316→			con.errorChan <- err
   317→			log.Errorf("ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v", con.peerAddr, con.conID, err)
   318→			TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()
   319→			return
   320→		}
   321→		// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.
   322→		if firstRequest {
   323→			// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request
   324→			if req.TypeUrl == model.HealthInfoType {
   325→				log.Warnf("ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request", con.peerAddr, con.conID)
   326→				continue
   327→			}
   328→			firstRequest = false
   329→			if req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == "" {
   330→				con.errorChan <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, "missing node information").Err()
   331→				return
   332→			}
   333→			if err := ctx.Initialize(req.Node); err != nil {
   334→				con.errorChan <- err
   335→				return
   336→			}
   337→			defer ctx.Close()
   338→			log.Infof("ADS: new connection for node:%s", con.conID)
   339→		}
   340→
   341→		select {
   342→		case con.reqChan <- req:
   343→		case <-con.stream.Context().Done():
   344→			log.Infof("ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed", con.peerAddr, con.conID)
   345→			return
   346→		}
   347→	}
   348→}
   349→
   350→// ShouldRespond determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol
   351→// using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.
   352→func ShouldRespond(w Watcher, id string, request *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) (bool, ResourceDelta) {
   353→	stype := model.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)
   354→
   355→	// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.
   356→	// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info
   357→	// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.
   358→	if request.ErrorDetail != nil {
   359→		errCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)
   360→		log.Warnf("ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s", stype, id, errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())
   361→		IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, w.GetID(), errCode.String())
   362→		w.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *WatchedResource) *WatchedResource {
   363→			wr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()
   364→			return wr
   365→		})
   366→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   367→	}
   368→
   369→	if shouldUnsubscribe(request) {
   370→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: UNSUBSCRIBE %s %s %s", stype, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   371→		w.DeleteWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)
   372→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   373→	}
   374→
   375→	previousInfo := w.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)
   376→	// This can happen in two cases:
   377→	// 1. When Envoy starts for the first time, it sends an initial Discovery request to Istiod.
   378→	// 2. When Envoy reconnects to a new Istiod that does not have information about this typeUrl
   379→	// i.e. non empty response nonce.
   380→	// We should always respond with the current resource names.
   381→	if request.ResponseNonce == "" || previousInfo == nil {
   382→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: INIT/RECONNECT %s %s %s", stype, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   383→		w.NewWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, request.ResourceNames)
   384→		return true, emptyResourceDelta
   385→	}
   386→
   387→	// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.
   388→	// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.
   389→	// previousInfo.NonceSent can be empty if we previously had shouldRespond=true but didn't send any resources.
   390→	if request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {
   391→		if features.EnableUnsafeAssertions && previousInfo.NonceSent == "" {
   392→			// Assert we do not end up in an invalid state
   393→			log.Fatalf("ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, but we never sent any nonce", stype,
   394→				id, request.ResponseNonce)
   395→		}
   396→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s", stype,
   397→			id, request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)
   398→		ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(model.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()
   399→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   400→	}
   401→
   402→	// If it comes here, that means nonce match.
   403→	var previousResources sets.String
   404→	var cur sets.String
   405→	var alwaysRespond bool
   406→	w.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *WatchedResource) *WatchedResource {
   407→		// Clear last error, we got an ACK.
   408→		wr.LastError = ""
   409→		previousResources = wr.ResourceNames
   410→		wr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce
   411→		wr.ResourceNames = sets.New(request.ResourceNames...)
   412→		cur = wr.ResourceNames
   413→		alwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond
   414→		wr.AlwaysRespond = false
   415→		return wr
   416→	})
   417→
   418→	// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce.
   419→	// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.
   420→	removed := previousResources.Difference(cur)
   421→	added := cur.Difference(previousResources)
   422→
   423→	// We should always respond "alwaysRespond" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming
   424→	// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.
   425→	if alwaysRespond {
   426→		log.Infof("ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.", stype, id)
   427→		return true, emptyResourceDelta
   428→	}
   429→
   430→	if len(removed) == 0 && len(added) == 0 {
   431→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: ACK %s %s %s", stype, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   432→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   433→	}
   434→	log.Debugf("ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE added %v removed %v %s %s %s", stype,
   435→		added, removed, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   436→
   437→	// For non wildcard resource, if no new resources are subscribed, it means we do not need to push.
   438→	if !IsWildcardTypeURL(request.TypeUrl) && len(added) == 0 {
   439→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   440→	}
   441→
   442→	return true, ResourceDelta{
   443→		Subscribed: added,
   444→		// we do not need to set unsubscribed for StoW
   445→	}
   446→}
   447→
   448→// shouldUnsubscribe checks if we should unsubscribe. This is done when Envoy is
   449→// no longer watching. For example, we remove all RDS references, we will
   450→// unsubscribe from RDS. NOTE: This may happen as part of the initial request. If
   451→// there are no routes needed, Envoy will send an empty request, which this
   452→// properly handles by not adding it to the watched resource list.
   453→func shouldUnsubscribe(request *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) bool {
   454→	return len(request.ResourceNames) == 0 && !IsWildcardTypeURL(request.TypeUrl)
   455→}
   456→
   457→func Send(ctx ConnectionContext, res *discovery.DiscoveryResponse) error {
   458→	conn := ctx.XdsConnection()
   459→	sendResponse := func() error {
   460→		start := time.Now()
   461→		defer func() { RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()
   462→		return conn.stream.Send(res)
   463→	}
   464→	err := sendResponse()
   465→	if err == nil {
   466→		if res.Nonce != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, model.DebugType) {
   467→			ctx.Watcher().UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *WatchedResource) *WatchedResource {
   468→				if wr == nil {
   469→					wr = &WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}
   470→				}
   471→				wr.NonceSent = res.Nonce
   472→				wr.LastSendTime = time.Now()
   473→				return wr
   474→			})
   475→		}
   476→	} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {
   477→		log.Infof("Timeout writing %s: %v", conn.conID, model.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))
   478→		ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()
   479→	}
   480→	return err
   481→}

*Connection) PushCh() chan any {\n\treturn conn.pushChannel\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) StopCh() chan struct{} {\n\treturn conn.stop\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) ErrorCh() chan error {\n\treturn conn.errorChan\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) StreamDone() <-chan struct{} {\n\treturn conn.stream.Context().Done()\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) ID() string {\n\treturn conn.conID\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Peer() string {\n\treturn conn.peerAddr\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) SetID(id string) {\n\tconn.conID = id\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) ConnectedAt() time.Time {\n\treturn conn.connectedAt\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Stop() {\n\tclose(conn.stop)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) MarkInitialized() {\n\tclose(conn.initialized)\n}\n\n// ConnectionContext is used by the RPC event loop to respond to requests and pushes.\ntype ConnectionContext interface {\n\tXdsConnection() *Connection\n\tWatcher() Watcher\n\t// Initialize checks the first request.\n\tInitialize(node *core.Node) error\n\t// Close discards the connection.\n\tClose()\n\t// Process responds to a discovery request.\n\tProcess(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error\n\t// Push responds to a push event queue\n\tPush(ev any) error\n}\n\nfunc Stream(ctx ConnectionContext) error {\n\tcon := ctx.XdsConnection()\n\t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). According to the spec: \"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\n\t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\"\n\n\t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\n\t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\n\tgo Receive(ctx)\n\n\t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to\n\t// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the\n\t// initialization is complete.\n\t<-con.initialized\n\n\tfor {\n\t\t// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.\n\t\t// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)\n\t\t// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase req, ok := <-con.reqChan:\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\tif err := ctx.Process(req); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n\t\t\t\treturn <-con.errorChan\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-con.stop:\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase req, ok := <-con.reqChan:\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\tif err := ctx.Process(req); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n\t\t\t\treturn <-con.errorChan\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase pushEv := <-con.pushChannel:\n\t\t\terr := ctx.Push(pushEv)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-con.stop:\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc Receive(ctx ConnectionContext) {\n\tcon := ctx.XdsConnection()\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tclose(con.errorChan)\n\t\tclose(con.reqChan)\n\t\t// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.initialized:\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tclose(con.initialized)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tfirstRequest := true\n\tfor {\n\t\treq, err := con.stream.Recv()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tif istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated\", con.peerAddr, con.conID)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcon.errorChan <- err\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v\", con.peerAddr, con.conID, err)\n\t\t\tTotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// This should be only set for the first request.     15→package xds
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"strings"
    19→	"time"
    20→
    21→	core "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3"
    22→	discovery "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3"
    23→	"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
    24→	"google.golang.org/grpc/status"

    26→
    27→	"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features"
    28→	istiogrpc "istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc"
    29→	"istio.io/istio/pkg/model"
    30→	"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets"
    31→)
    32→
    33→// ResourceDelta records the difference in requested resources by an XDS client
    34→type ResourceDelta struct {
    35→	// Subscribed indicates the client requested these additional resources
    36→	Subscribed sets.String
    37→	// Unsubscribed indicates the client no longer requires these resources
    38→	Unsubscribed sets.String
    39→}
    40→
    41→var emptyResourceDelta = ResourceDelta{}
    42→
    43→func (rd ResourceDelta) IsEmpty() bool {
    44→	return len(rd.Subscribed) == 0 && len(rd.Unsubscribed) == 0
    45→}
    46→
    47→type Resources = []*discovery.Resource
    48→
    49→func ResourcesToAny(r Resources) []*anypb.Any {
    50→	a := make([]*anypb.Any, 0, len(r))
    51→	for _, rr := range r {
    52→		a = append(a, rr.Resource)
    53→	}
    54→	return a
    55→}
    56→
    57→// WatchedResource tracks an active DiscoveryRequest subscription.
    58→type WatchedResource struct {
    59→	// TypeUrl is copied from the DiscoveryRequest.TypeUrl that initiated watching this resource.
    60→	// nolint
    61→	TypeUrl string
    62→
    63→	// ResourceNames tracks the list of resources that are actively watched.
    64→	// For LDS and CDS, all resources of the TypeUrl type are watched if it is empty.
    65→	// For endpoints the resource names will have list of clusters and for clusters it is empty.
    66→	// For Delta Xds, all resources of the TypeUrl that a client has subscribed to.
    67→	ResourceNames sets.String
    68→
    69→	// Wildcard indicates the subscription is a wildcard subscription. This only applies to types that
    70→	// allow both wildcard and non-wildcard subscriptions.
    71→	Wildcard bool
    72→
    73→	// NonceSent is the nonce sent in the last sent response. If it is equal with NonceAcked, the
    74→	// last message has been processed. If empty: we never sent a message of this type.
    75→	NonceSent string
    76→
    77→	// NonceAcked is the last acked message.
    78→	NonceAcked string
    79→
    80→	// AlwaysRespond, if true, will ensure that even when a request would otherwise be treated as an
    81→	// ACK, it will be responded to. This typically happens when a proxy reconnects to another instance of
    82→	// Istiod. In that case, Envoy expects us to respond to EDS/RDS/SDS requests to finish warming of
    83→	// clusters/listeners.
    84→	// Typically, this should be set to 'false' after response; keeping it true would likely result in an endless loop.
    85→	AlwaysRespond bool
    86→
    87→	// LastSendTime tracks the last time we sent a message. This should change every time NonceSent changes.
    88→	LastSendTime time.Time
    89→
    90→	// LastError records the last error returned, if any. This is cleared on any successful ACK.
    91→	LastError string
    92→
    93→	// LastResources tracks the contents of the last push.
    94→	// This field is extremely expensive to maintain and is typically disabled
    95→	LastResources Resources
    96→}
    97→
    98→type Watcher interface {
    99→	DeleteWatchedResource(url string)
   100→	GetWatchedResource(url string) *WatchedResource
   101→	NewWatchedResource(url string, names []string)
   102→	UpdateWatchedResource(string, func(*WatchedResource) *WatchedResource)
   103→	// GetID identifies an xDS client. This is different from a connection ID.
   104→	GetID() string
   105→}
   106→
   107→// IsWildcardTypeURL checks whether a given type is a wildcard type
   108→// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#how-the-client-specifies-what-resources-to-return
   109→// If the list of resource names becomes empty, that means that the client is no
   110→// longer interested in any resources of the specified type. For Listener and
   111→// Cluster resource types, there is also a “wildcard” mode, which is triggered
   112→// when the initial request on the stream for that resource type contains no
   113→// resource names.
   114→func IsWildcardTypeURL(typeURL string) bool {
   115→	switch typeURL {
   116→	case model.SecretType, model.EndpointType, model.RouteType, model.ExtensionConfigurationType:
   117→		// By XDS spec, these are not wildcard
   118→		return false
   119→	case model.ClusterType, model.ListenerType:
   120→		// By XDS spec, these are wildcard
   121→		return true
   122→	default:
   123→		// All of our internal types use wildcard semantics
   124→		return true
   125→	}
   126→}
   127→
   128→// DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.
   129→type DiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesServer
   130→
   131→// Connection holds information about an xDS client connection. There may be more than one connection to the same client.
   132→type Connection struct {
   133→	// peerAddr is the address of the client, from network layer.
   134→	peerAddr string
   135→
   136→	// Time of connection, for debugging
   137→	connectedAt time.Time
   138→
   139→	// conID is the connection conID, used as a key in the connection table.
   140→	// Currently based on the node name and a counter.
   141→	conID string
   142→
   143→	// Sending on this channel results in a push.
   144→	pushChannel chan any
   145→
   146→	// Both ADS and SDS streams implement this interface
   147→	stream DiscoveryStream
   148→
   149→	// initialized channel will be closed when proxy is initialized. Pushes, or anything accessing
   150→	// the proxy, should not be started until this channel is closed.
   151→	initialized chan struct{}
   152→
   153→	// stop can be used to end the connection manually via debug endpoints. Only to be used for testing.
   154→	stop chan struct{}
   155→
   156→	// reqChan is used to receive discovery requests for this connection.
   157→	reqChan chan *discovery.DiscoveryRequest
   158→
   159→	// errorChan is used to process error during discovery request processing.
   160→	errorChan chan error
   161→}
   162→
   163→func NewConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) Connection {
   164→	return Connection{
   165→		pushChannel: make(chan any),
   166→		initialized: make(chan struct{}),
   167→		stop:        make(chan struct{}),
   168→		reqChan:     make(chan *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, 1),
   169→		errorChan:   make(chan error, 1),
   170→		peerAddr:    peerAddr,
   171→		connectedAt: time.Now(),
   172→		stream:      stream,
   173→	}
   174→}
   175→
   176→func (conn *Connection) InitializedCh() chan struct{} {
   177→	return conn.initialized
   178→}
   179→
   180→func (conn *Connection) PushCh() chan any {
   181→	return conn.pushChannel
   182→}
   183→
   184→func (conn *Connection) StopCh() chan struct{} {
   185→	return conn.stop
   186→}
   187→
   188→func (conn *Connection) ErrorCh() chan error {
   189→	return conn.errorChan
   190→}
   191→
   192→func (conn *Connection) StreamDone() <-chan struct{} {
   193→	return conn.stream.Context().Done()
   194→}
   195→
   196→func (conn *Connection) ID() string {
   197→	return conn.conID
   198→}
   199→
   200→func (conn *Connection) Peer() string {
   201→	return conn.peerAddr
   202→}
   203→
   204→func (conn *Connection) SetID(id string) {
   205→	conn.conID = id
   206→}
   207→
   208→func (conn *Connection) ConnectedAt() time.Time {
   209→	return conn.connectedAt
   210→}
   211→
   212→func (conn *Connection) Stop() {
   213→	close(conn.stop)
   214→}
   215→
   216→func (conn *Connection) MarkInitialized() {
   217→	close(conn.initialized)
   218→}
   219→
   220→// ConnectionContext is used by the RPC event loop to respond to requests and pushes.
   221→type ConnectionContext interface {
   222→	XdsConnection() *Connection
   223→	Watcher() Watcher
   224→	// Initialize checks the first request.
   225→	Initialize(node *core.Node) error
   226→	// Close discards the connection.
   227→	Close()
   228→	// Process responds to a discovery request.
   229→	Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error
   230→	// Push responds to a push event queue
   231→	Push(ev any) error
   232→}
   233→
   234→func Stream(ctx ConnectionContext) error {
   235→	con := ctx.XdsConnection()
   236→	// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected
   237→	// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions
   238→	// with push. According to the spec: "It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important
   239→	// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent."
   240→
   241→	// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.
   242→	// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().
   243→	go Receive(ctx)
   244→
   245→	// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because
   246→	// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout
   247→	// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to
   248→	// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the
   249→	// initialization is complete.
   250→	<-con.initialized
   251→
   252→	for {
   253→		// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.
   254→		// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)
   255→		// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.
   256→		select {
   257→		case req, ok := <-con.reqChan:
   258→			if ok {
   259→				if err := ctx.Process(req); err != nil {
   260→					return err
   261→				}
   262→			} else {
   263→				// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.
   264→				return <-con.errorChan
   265→			}
   266→		case <-con.stop:
   267→			return nil
   268→		default:
   269→		}
   270→		// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge
   271→		// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;
   272→		// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests
   273→		// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.
   274→		select {
   275→		case req, ok := <-con.reqChan:
   276→			if ok {
   277→				if err := ctx.Process(req); err != nil {
   278→					return err
   279→				}
   280→			} else {
   281→				// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.
   282→				return <-con.errorChan
   283→			}
   284→		case pushEv := <-con.pushChannel:
   285→			err := ctx.Push(pushEv)
   286→			if err != nil {
   287→				return err
   288→			}
   289→		case <-con.stop:
   290→			return nil
   291→		}
   292→	}
   293→}
   294→
   295→func Receive(ctx ConnectionContext) {
   296→	con := ctx.XdsConnection()
   297→	defer func() {
   298→		close(con.errorChan)
   299→		close(con.reqChan)
   300→		// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream.
   301→		select {
   302→		case <-con.initialized:
   303→		default:
   304→			close(con.initialized)
   305→		}
   306→	}()
   307→
   308→	firstRequest := true
   309→	for {
   310→		req, err := con.stream.Recv()
   311→		if err != nil {
   312→			if istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {
   313→				log.Infof("ADS: %q %s terminated", con.peerAddr, con.conID)
   314→				return
   315→			}
   316→			con.errorChan <- err
   317→			log.Errorf("ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v", con.peerAddr, con.conID, err)
   318→			TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()
   319→			return
   320→		}
   321→		// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.
   322→		if firstRequest {
   323→			// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request
   324→			if req.TypeUrl == model.HealthInfoType {
   325→				log.Warnf("ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request", con.peerAddr, con.conID)
   326→				continue
   327→			}
   328→			firstRequest = false
   329→			if req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == "" {
   330→				con.errorChan <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, "missing node information").Err()
   331→				return
   332→			}
   333→			if err := ctx.Initialize(req.Node); err != nil {
   334→				con.errorChan <- err
   335→				return
   336→			}
   337→			defer ctx.Close()
   338→			log.Infof("ADS: new connection for node:%s", con.conID)
   339→		}
   340→
   341→		select {
   342→		case con.reqChan <- req:
   343→		case <-con.stream.Context().Done():
   344→			log.Infof("ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed", con.peerAddr, con.conID)
   345→			return
   346→		}
   347→	}
   348→}
   349→
   350→// ShouldRespond determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol
   351→// using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.
   352→func ShouldRespond(w Watcher, id string, request *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) (bool, ResourceDelta) {
   353→	stype := model.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)
   354→
   355→	// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.
   356→	// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info
   357→	// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.
   358→	if request.ErrorDetail != nil {
   359→		errCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)
   360→		log.Warnf("ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s", stype, id, errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())
   361→		IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, w.GetID(), errCode.String())
   362→		w.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *WatchedResource) *WatchedResource {
   363→			wr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()
   364→			return wr
   365→		})
   366→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   367→	}
   368→
   369→	if shouldUnsubscribe(request) {
   370→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: UNSUBSCRIBE %s %s %s", stype, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   371→		w.DeleteWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)
   372→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   373→	}
   374→
   375→	previousInfo := w.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)
   376→	// This can happen in two cases:
   377→	// 1. When Envoy starts for the first time, it sends an initial Discovery request to Istiod.
   378→	// 2. When Envoy reconnects to a new Istiod that does not have information about this typeUrl
   379→	// i.e. non empty response nonce.
   380→	// We should always respond with the current resource names.
   381→	if request.ResponseNonce == "" || previousInfo == nil {
   382→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: INIT/RECONNECT %s %s %s", stype, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   383→		w.NewWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, request.ResourceNames)
   384→		return true, emptyResourceDelta
   385→	}
   386→
   387→	// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.
   388→	// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.
   389→	// previousInfo.NonceSent can be empty if we previously had shouldRespond=true but didn't send any resources.
   390→	if request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {
   391→		if features.EnableUnsafeAssertions && previousInfo.NonceSent == "" {
   392→			// Assert we do not end up in an invalid state
   393→			log.Fatalf("ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, but we never sent any nonce", stype,
   394→				id, request.ResponseNonce)
   395→		}
   396→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s", stype,
   397→			id, request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)
   398→		ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(model.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()
   399→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   400→	}
   401→
   402→	// If it comes here, that means nonce match.
   403→	var previousResources sets.String
   404→	var cur sets.String
   405→	var alwaysRespond bool
   406→	w.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *WatchedResource) *WatchedResource {
   407→		// Clear last error, we got an ACK.
   408→		wr.LastError = ""
   409→		previousResources = wr.ResourceNames
   410→		wr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce
   411→		wr.ResourceNames = sets.New(request.ResourceNames...)
   412→		cur = wr.ResourceNames
   413→		alwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond
   414→		wr.AlwaysRespond = false
   415→		return wr
   416→	})
   417→
   418→	// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce.
   419→	// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.
   420→	removed := previousResources.Difference(cur)
   421→	added := cur.Difference(previousResources)
   422→
   423→	// We should always respond "alwaysRespond" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming
   424→	// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.
   425→	if alwaysRespond {
   426→		log.Infof("ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.", stype, id)
   427→		return true, emptyResourceDelta
   428→	}
   429→
   430→	if len(removed) == 0 && len(added) == 0 {
   431→		log.Debugf("ADS:%s: ACK %s %s %s", stype, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   432→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   433→	}
   434→	log.Debugf("ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE added %v removed %v %s %s %s", stype,
   435→		added, removed, id, request.VersionInfo, request.ResponseNonce)
   436→
   437→	// For non wildcard resource, if no new resources are subscribed, it means we do not need to push.
   438→	if !IsWildcardTypeURL(request.TypeUrl) && len(added) == 0 {
   439→		return false, emptyResourceDelta
   440→	}
   441→
   442→	return true, ResourceDelta{
   443→		Subscribed: added,
   444→		// we do not need to set unsubscribed for StoW
   445→	}
   446→}
   447→
   448→// shouldUnsubscribe checks if we should unsubscribe. This is done when Envoy is
   449→// no longer watching. For example, we remove all RDS references, we will
   450→// unsubscribe from RDS. NOTE: This may happen as part of the initial request. If
   451→// there are no routes needed, Envoy will send an empty request, which this
   452→// properly handles by not adding it to the watched resource list.
   453→func shouldUnsubscribe(request *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) bool {
   454→	return len(request.ResourceNames) == 0 && !IsWildcardTypeURL(request.TypeUrl)
   455→}
   456→
   457→func Send(ctx ConnectionContext, res *discovery.DiscoveryResponse) error {
   458→	conn := ctx.XdsConnection()
   459→	sendResponse := func() error {
   460→		start := time.Now()
   461→		defer func() { RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()
   462→		return conn.stream.Send(res)
   463→	}
   464→	err := sendResponse()
   465→	if err == nil {
   466→		if res.Nonce != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, model.DebugType) {
   467→			ctx.Watcher().UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *WatchedResource) *WatchedResource {
   468→				if wr == nil {
   469→					wr = &WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}
   470→				}
   471→				wr.NonceSent = res.Nonce
   472→				wr.LastSendTime = time.Now()
   473→				return wr
   474→			})
   475→		}
   476→	} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {
   477→		log.Infof("Timeout writing %s: %v", conn.conID, model.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))
   478→		ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()
   479→	}
   480→	return err
   481→}

    33→// ResourceDelta records the difference in requested resources by an XDS client
    34→type ResourceDelta struct {
    35→	// Subscribed indicates the client requested these additional resources
    36→	Subscribed sets.String
    37→	// Unsubscribed indicates the client no longer requires these resources
    38→	Unsubscribed sets.String
    39→}
    40→
    41→var emptyResourceDelta = ResourceDelta{}
    42→
    43→func (rd ResourceDelta) IsEmpty() bool {
    44→	return len(rd.Subscribed) == 0 && len(rd.Unsubscribed) == 0
    45→}
    46→
    47→type Resources = []*discovery.Resource
    48→
    49→func ResourcesToAny(r Resources) []*anypb.Any {
    50→	a := make([]*anypb.Any, 0, len(r))
    51→	for _, rr := range r {
    52→		a = append(a, rr.Resource)
    53→	}
    54→	return a
    55→}
    56→
    57→// WatchedResource tracks an active DiscoveryRequest subscription.
    58→type WatchedResource struct {
    59→	// TypeUrl is copied from the DiscoveryRequest.TypeUrl that initiated watching this resource.
    60→	// nolint
    61→	TypeUrl string
    62→
    63→	// ResourceNames tracks the list of resources that are actively watched.
    64→	// For LDS and CDS, all resources of the TypeUrl type are watched if it is empty.
    65→	// For endpoints the resource names will have list of clusters and for clusters it is empty.
    66→	// For Delta Xds, all resources of the TypeUrl that a client has subscribed to.
    67→	ResourceNames sets.String
    68→
    69→	// Wildcard indicates the subscription is a wildcard subscription. This only applies to types that
    70→	// allow both wildcard and non-wildcard subscriptions.
    71→	Wildcard bool
    72→
    73→	// NonceSent is the nonce sent in the last sent response. If it is equal with NonceAcked, the
    74→	// last message has been processed. If empty: we never sent a message of this type.
    75→	NonceSent string
    76→
    77→	// NonceAcked is the last acked message.
    78→	NonceAcked string
    79→
    80→	// AlwaysRespond, if true, will ensure that even when a request would otherwise be treated as an
    81→	// ACK, it will be responded to. This typically happens when a proxy reconnects to another instance of
    82→	// Istiod. In that case, Envoy expects us to respond to EDS/RDS/SDS requests to finish warming of
    83→	// clusters/listeners.
    84→	// Typically, this should be set to 'false' after response; keeping it true would likely result in an endless loop.
    85→	AlwaysRespond bool
    86→
    87→	// LastSendTime tracks the last time we sent a message. This should change every time NonceSent changes.
    88→	LastSendTime time.Time
    89→
    90→	// LastError records the last error returned, if any. This is cleared on any successful ACK.
    91→	LastError string
    92→
    93→	// LastResources tracks the contents of the last push.
    94→	// This field is extremely expensive to maintain and is typically disabled
    95→	LastResources Resources
    96→}
    97→
    98→type Watcher interface {
    99→	DeleteWatchedResource(url string)
   100→	GetWatchedResource(url string) *WatchedResource
   101→	NewWatchedResource(url string, names []string)
   102→	UpdateWatchedResource(string, func(*WatchedResource) *WatchedResource)
   103→	// GetID identifies an xDS client. This is different from a connection ID.
   104→	GetID() string
   105→}
   106→
   107→// IsWildcardTypeURL checks whether a given type is a wildcard type
   108→// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#how-the-client-specifies-what-resources-to-return
   109→// If the list of resource names becomes empty, that means that the client is no
   110→// longer interested in any resources of the specified type. For Listener and
   111→// Cluster resource types, there is also a “wildcard” mode, which is triggered
   112→// when the initial request on the stream for that resource type contains no
   113→// resource names.
   114→func IsWildcardTypeURL(typeURL string) bool {
   115→	switch typeURL {
   116→	case model.SecretType, model.EndpointType, model.RouteType, model.ExtensionConfigurationType:
   117→		// By XDS spec, these are not wildcard
   118→		return false
   119→	case model.ClusterType, model.ListenerType:
   120→		// By XDS spec, these are wildcard
   121→		return true
   122→	default:
   123→		// All of our internal types use wildcard semantics
   124→		return true
   125→	}
   126→}
